questions.explanation: You/we/they take were in the past: They were at school. I/he/she/it take was. Are is the present, and been is the past participle (used after have), not the simple past.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Yesterday I ___ football with my friends."
questions.explanation: Regular verbs add -ed: play → played. Play ends in a vowel + y, so we keep the y and simply add -ed (only consonant + y becomes -ied, like study → studied). "playd" and "plaied" are misspellings.

questions.prompt: Complete: "She ___ to the market this morning."
questions.explanation: go is irregular — its past is went, with no -ed: She went to the market. "goed" wrongly applies the regular rule, "gone" is the past participle (have gone), and "goes" is the present tense.

questions.prompt: Complete the negative: "He ___ his homework last night."
questions.explanation: Negatives use didn't + the BASE verb: He didn't do his homework. The past is already carried by didn't, so the main verb stays as the base form do — never "didn't did" or "didn't done".

questions.prompt: Complete the question: "___ you see the film last weekend?"
questions.explanation: Past questions begin with Did + subject + base verb: Did you see the film? Was/Were ask about the verb to be, not about an action, and Do is the present tense, not the past.
